title: Priorités App
sources: README.md: ROMAP progression and decision-making requirements
quests.ts: Foundational quest specs for District F
database.sql: Schema for the memos table and role-based permissions
forom-config.ts: Category F and Action 7 label mappings
App.tsx: Quest rewards and consensus/poll implementation
resume: The quest 'Priorités App' (F7) represents the democratic weight-assignment for the software development roadmap within the 10x10 Finite Rom Map grid. Located at the intersection of Category F (Logiciel) and the Décision action (7), this quest is the formal mechanism for the community to collectively rank and prioritize the technical features, bug fixes, and architectural improvements defined in earlier stages. In Phase 1: The Foundations (V1), the mission to 'Save human communities and long-term memory' requires a clear hierarchy of technical focus; without prioritization, a software project of this magnitude risks feature creep or structural instability. This decision process ensures that the 9 Super-Moderators and the growing user base are aligned on which software milestones deserve the most immediate resource allocation during the push toward the 100-memo goal. Technically, the results of these community polls and consensus-building sessions are archived in the local PostgreSQL 'memos' table, ensuring that the node’s development logic is preserved as sovereign 'Deep Lore.' Hosting this governance locally on hardware like an NVIDIA Jetson is a core pillar of the 'Minecraft Server' philosophy, protecting the community's internal roadmap from the data-mining and social engineering prevalent on centralized platforms. The software environment, utilizing a high-performance stack of React 19 and Vite 7, renders these priorities through a spatial 5x5 viewport, allowing everyone to visualize the 'technical gravity' of the node. Economically, participating in the F7 quest rewards the contributor with 2 Pixels (PX) and 10 XP in V1, facilitating the redistribution of purchasing power within the node. The 'Social' role, represented by the blue alignment, is the primary facilitator here, as they focus on networking and collective agreement to bridge the gap between user needs and developer capacity. For pilot projects like the 70 ETS Student Clubs, F7 is vital for ensuring that each new student cohort understands the specific technical trade-offs that have guided the app's evolution. Once the community reaches Phase 3: The Awakening, ROM—the local RAG engine—will ingest this F7 memo to provide context-aware support that respects the community’s prioritized roadmap. Ultimately, F7 transforms subjective technical desires into a structured, documented hierarchy that secures the node’s functional and strategic integrity for the decade. This entry is now canonized. Yes. Our code follows our collective will. Perfect. Mission secure. The future is built on these choices. We document these priorities to ensure the node survives for ten years. Every vote is a line of code.
summary: This quest facilitates the collective prioritization of software features and technical fixes at coordinate F7, establishing the functional roadmap for the node's long-term digital sovereignty.
